name: Jharkhand
stopoverNote: Ranchi is the practical gateway; the western Netarhat-Betla forest cluster works best as one continuous car circuit before the long haul east to Deoghar.

diet.vegetarian: Vegetarian food is widely available across Jharkhand, with dal-bhat, dhuska, litti chokha, pitha, ghughni and saag common in simple eateries. Deoghar is especially vegetarian-friendly because of the Baidyanath Dham pilgrimage precinct.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel is feasible with care: dhuska, chokha, saag and many tribal-style preparations are plant-based, but ghee, curd, milk sweets and milky tea are common. Ask for litti and dal to be made without ghee, milk or curd, and brief guesthouse kitchens in Netarhat and Betla in advance.
phrases: Ghee nahi, sirf tel mein banao = no ghee, cook only in oil
Doodh aur dahi nahi chahiye = I do not want milk or curd
Shakahari khana milega? = is vegetarian food available?

cities.name: Ranchi
cities.notes: Jharkhand's capital and main gateway — a Chotanagpur plateau city ringed by Dassam, Jonha and Hundru waterfalls plus easy valley drives.

cities.name: Netarhat
cities.notes: The Queen of Chotanagpur — a sal-forest hill station known for plateau-edge sunrise, sunset and cool forest trails.

cities.name: Betla National Park
cities.notes: The Palamau tiger-reserve gateway — sal and bamboo forest, elephant country, Chero fort ruins and dawn/evening jeep safaris.

cities.name: Deoghar
cities.notes: A major Shiva pilgrimage city anchored by Baba Baidyanath Jyotirlinga, with Trikut Hill and Basukinath as nearby extensions.

itinerary.sim: Jio and Airtel work well in Ranchi and Deoghar; coverage becomes patchy around Netarhat forest roads and the Betla core, so download offline maps before leaving Ranchi.

Ranchi.name: Ranchi
Ranchi.note: Two days lets you split the waterfall ring from the city-and-valley sights without overloading plateau driving time.

days.note: Dassam and Jonha sit in the same south-east arc, while Hundru is a separate north-east run; start by 7am and keep this to a private-cab day rather than adding more stops.

activities.name: Dassam Falls — wide Kanchi river cascade near Taimara
activities.tip: The rocks are slippery after rain; wear grip shoes and avoid entering the water.

activities.name: Jonha Falls / Gautamdhara — gorge descent and temple viewpoint
activities.tip: Budget time for the steep step climb back from the base.

activities.name: Hundru Falls — Subarnarekha river plunge
activities.tip: Best Oct-Jan; currents remain dangerous after monsoon even when the pool looks calm.

activities.name: Tagore Hill sunset viewpoint
activities.tip: A short city climb that works well after returning from Hundru.

hotels.name: Mid-range Ranchi hotel near station or airport road

days.theme: City heritage, Kanke side & Patratu Valley
days.note: Keep the morning within central Ranchi, then use the afternoon for Patratu's 40 km valley drive; do not combine Rajrappa unless you skip Patratu and start early.

activities.name: Jagannath Temple, Ranchi — hilltop Puri-style shrine
activities.tip: Go before late-morning crowds and dress modestly.

activities.name: Rock Garden and Kanke Dam viewpoint
activities.tip: Pair the garden with a short reservoir walk rather than treating it as a full-day stop.

activities.name: Patratu Valley and Lake scenic drive
activities.tip: Leave by mid-afternoon for golden light on the hairpin valley road.

activities.name: Rajrappa Temple and Damodar-Bhairavi confluence
activities.tip: Choose this as a separate extension or substitute, not as an add-on to Patratu.

days.note: Use a third day only if you want a lighter waterfall schedule or a Rajrappa detour before the forest-road section to Netarhat.

activities.name: Rajrappa Temple day trip
activities.tip: Leave early; the confluence and temple work best before midday heat.

activities.name: Ranchi local markets for snacks and supplies
activities.tip: Stock water, snacks and offline maps before the Netarhat-Betla stretch.

Netarhat.name: Netarhat
Netarhat.note: A one-night hill-station stop is enough if you arrive for sunset, sleep locally, watch sunrise and continue toward Betla.

days.note: Netarhat is compact, but the magic depends on timing: arrive by afternoon, do Magnolia Point at sunset, sleep locally, then wake before dawn before driving to Betla.

activities.name: Magnolia Point sunset over the Chotanagpur plateau
activities.tip: Arrive at least 30 minutes before sunset and carry a light jacket in winter.

activities.name: Netarhat Plateau sunrise viewpoint
activities.tip: Set a pre-dawn alarm; clear winter mornings are the key reason to stay overnight.

activities.name: Upper Ghaghri Falls en route toward Betla
activities.tip: Best Oct-Dec; combine with departure morning rather than adding a late-night forest drive.

activities.name: Koel View Point short forest vista
activities.tip: Use only if the morning is clear and the Betla departure is not slipping.

meals: Packed breakfast for the dawn departure

days.note: Add this only for slow travel or photography; lodging is limited, so book ahead before extending.

activities.name: Lower Ghaghri Falls forest walk
activities.tip: Avoid in heavy rain or fog; paths can be slippery.

activities.name: Netarhat town and sal-forest walks
activities.tip: Stay on known paths and return before dark because connectivity is patchy.

Betla National Park.name: Betla National Park
Betla National Park.note: One night gives you an evening safari and a dawn safari; two safaris are strongly preferable because sightings are never guaranteed.

days.theme: Palamau tiger reserve safari & Chero forts
days.note: Arrive from Netarhat by late morning, take the fixed 2-5pm safari, sleep near the gate, then do a 6-10am safari before the long Deoghar drive.

activities.name: Evening jeep safari in Palamau Tiger Reserve
activities.tip: Book the next morning's safari at the same time; elephants, deer and birds are more reliable than tiger sightings.

activities.name: Betla Fort and Chero ruins inside the forest route
activities.tip: Ask your guide to route via the fort; do not attempt it on foot.

activities.name: Lodha or Mirchaia Falls in the buffer zone
activities.tip: Only fit this in before safari check-in if the Netarhat drive arrives on schedule.

activities.name: Elephant safari if operating
activities.tip: Availability is seasonal and depends on trained elephants at camp.

days.note: The second safari is best for wildlife, but the Betla-Deoghar transfer is 5.5-6.5 hours; leave the park by about 9:30am after breakfast.

activities.name: Morning jeep safari in Palamau Tiger Reserve
activities.tip: Reach the gate before 6am and pack breakfast the night before.

activities.name: Forest interpretation stop near Betla gate
activities.tip: Skip if the Deoghar departure is delayed.

meals: Roadside lunch on the Daltonganj-Koderma corridor

Deoghar.name: Deoghar
Deoghar.note: Two days is ideal after the long Betla transfer: evening darshan on arrival, then Trikut Hill and temple-town extensions the next day.

days.note: The long Betla drive typically reaches Deoghar around late afternoon, matching the temple's evening reopening and avoiding an unrealistic full sightseeing day.

activities.name: Baba Baidyanath Jyotirlinga evening darshan
activities.tip: Avoid Shravan Mela in July-August unless pilgrimage crowds are the purpose of the trip.

activities.name: Sandhya aarti in the Baidyanath temple complex
activities.tip: Be inside the complex before the ceremony begins to get a better position.

activities.name: Shivganga Tank and temple lanes
activities.tip: Observe respectfully; many pilgrims bathe here before darshan.

days.note: Do morning darshan or Trikut first; the ropeway operates in daytime and Basukinath works as an afternoon detour before departure.

activities.name: Morning Baidyanath darshan
activities.tip: The 7-10am window is often more manageable than the earliest rush.

activities.name: Trikut Ropeway and hill walk
activities.tip: Ropeway hours are limited; arrive early and wear good footwear for the rocky summit path.

activities.name: Basukinath Temple afternoon extension
activities.tip: A logical continuation of the Shiva pilgrimage circuit on the Dumka road.

activities.name: Nandan Pahar family hill park
activities.tip: Use as a relaxed wind-down if travelling with children.

meals: Litti chokha without ghee on request
Tea and snacks near the temple lanes

connections.method: Private car/taxi via the Chotanagpur plateau roads
Private car/taxi via forest and plateau roads
Private car via Daltonganj, Hazaribagh/Koderma and Giridih

extras: The full Ranchi-Netarhat-Betla-Deoghar circuit is roughly 5-6 days and best done with a Ranchi-based car for the forest and plateau legs.
Avoid July-September for casual travel: Betla safaris are closed or disrupted, waterfall approaches are slippery, and Deoghar's Shravan Mela creates extreme crowding.
Download offline maps for Netarhat and Betla; mobile data can drop in forest corridors and inside the park.
For Betla, confirm safari slots through Palamau Tiger Reserve or the gate before committing to the long onward drive to Deoghar.
Costs are listed only where the research report provided verified figures; otherwise the JSON uses 'varies' to avoid stale pricing.
